Title: Engineering yeast for the production of breviscapine by genomic analysis and synthetic biology approaches

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled in vivo supply of UDP-glucuronic acid for EbF7GAT-mediated production of apigenin-7-O-glucuronide in yeast
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog-based identification; co-expression in engineered yeast with EbF7GAT; metabolite profiling by HPLC and LC-MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EbUDPGDH was identified from E. breviscapus based on an ortholog from Glycine max. 2. Co-expression of EbUDPGDH with EbF7GAT in apigenin-producing yeast enabled formation of apigenin-7-O-glucuronide in vivo. 3. It functioned as precursor-supplying gene by providing UDP-glucuronic acid for glucuronidation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in engineered yeast during fermentation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene to support flavonoid glucuronide production in microbial engineering systems
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome re-annotation; RNA-seq from leaves of wild and cultivated individuals and multiple tissues of a cultivated individual
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not itself the focal catalytic step to terminal flavonoid scaffold modification, but required in vivo to supply sugar donor for EbF7GAT.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provided electron transfer support required for EbF6H catalytic activity in yeast and microsomes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with candidate P450s in yeast; microsomal enzyme assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EbCPR was cloned from E. breviscapus as an electron donor partner for P450 catalysis. 2. Co-expression with CYP706X/EbF6H enabled hydroxylation of apigenin to scutellarein and downstream scutellarin production. 3. It served as an essential redox partner rather than a direct product-forming enzyme.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in engineered yeast during fermentation and microsome preparation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Breviscapine components are used for treatment of cardio- and cerebrovascular diseases
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxygen availability affected interpretation because EbF6H is a P450 requiring molecular oxygen
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential partner for reconstructing plant P450-dependent flavonoid pathways in heterologous hosts
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome re-annotation; RNA-seq-assisted candidate selection context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Support protein for P450 screening; not the primary research target metabolite-determining gene.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid from UDP-glucuronic acid to the 7-O position of apigenin or scutellarein
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway enzyme identified in this study; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major active ingredients of breviscapine used to treat cardio- and cerebrovascular diseases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of apigenin-7-O-glucuronide directly and of scutellarin when combined with EbF6H
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic family assignment; purified recombinant enzyme assay; kinetics; substrate spectrum assay; heterologous expression in yeast; HPLC and LC-MS metabolite identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EbF7GAT was identified as the sole UGT88-family gene in E. breviscapus and functionally validated as a flavonoid 7-O-glucuronosyltransferase. 2. Purified EbF7GAT glucuronidated apigenin and scutellarein in vitro, with higher catalytic efficiency toward apigenin than scutellarein. 3. Co-expression with EbUDPGDH in apigenin-producing yeast generated apigenin-7-O-glucuronide in vivo. 4. Together with EbF6H, it completed scutellarin biosynthesis in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day shake-flask fermentation; fed-batch fermentation from glucose
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme for microbial production of flavonoid glucuronides and tunable breviscapine composition engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome re-annotation; RNA-seq-assisted annotation; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Apigenin-7-O-glucuronide produced in yeast was ~26.6% intracellular and 73.4% secreted after 5 days.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream hydroxylation step before terminal glucuronidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates apigenin at the 6-position to form scutellarein
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway enzyme identified in this study; oxygen-dependent P450 control point
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed candidate among P450 genes in E. breviscapus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in engineered yeast and yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major active ingredient of breviscapine used to treat cardio- and cerebrovascular diseases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes scutellarin production by converting apigenin to scutellarein, which is then glucuronidated by EbF7GAT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics and transcriptomics candidate selection; heterologous screening of 36 P450 genes in yeast; LC-MS; scutellarein structural confirmation by NMR; microsomal enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP706X was identified from 36 highly expressed P450 candidates as the only gene producing scutellarin in the engineered yeast screen. 2. Expression with EbCPR converted apigenin to scutellarein in vivo and in microsomal assays. 3. EbF6H could not hydroxylate apigenin-7-O-glucuronide, establishing the pathway order as apigenin to scutellarein to scutellarin. 4. Dissolved oxygen was important for product ratio because EbF6H is a P450 enzyme requiring molecular oxygen.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dissolved oxygen concentration in fed-batch fermentation; 5-day plate and flask fermentation; NADPH-dependent microsomal assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical hydroxylase for reconstructing scutellarin biosynthesis and for tuning scutellarin/apigenin-7-O-glucuronide ratio in fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product formation is sensitive to oxygen supply due to P450 dependence
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome re-annotation; RNA-seq-assisted expression prioritization; comparative genomics across Asteraceae and outgroups; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to a distinct CYP706X lineage rather than previously reported CYP82D/CYP71D9 F6Hs; orthologs in other Asteraceae also showed F6H activity.
